skill.name: Team Composition Analysis
skill.description: Design optimal team structures, hiring plans, compensation strategies, and equity allocation for early-stage startups from pre-seed through Series A
skill.summary: Plan team structure and hiring for startups with compensation benchmarks and equity allocation guidance
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. No security risks detected. The skill provides guidance for team composition, hiring planning, compensation benchmarks, and equity allocation for early-stage startups. No file system access, network operations, external commands, or environment variable access present.

content.user_title: Plan Startup Team Structure and Hiring
content.value_statement: Build the right team at the right time with data-driven hiring plans, compensation benchmarks, and equity allocation strategies. Scale from pre-seed to Series A with role-by-role guidance and budget planning tools.
actual_capabilities: Design team structures for pre-seed through Series A startups
Create role-by-role hiring plans with timeline estimates
Calculate fully-loaded compensation costs including benefits and taxes
Provide equity allocation benchmarks by role and funding stage
Generate organizational reporting structures and span of control guidance
Budget headcount costs aligned with revenue milestones
limitations: Benchmarks based on US market rates from 2024, may vary by location
Equity ranges are general guidelines, actual allocations depend on company specifics
Hiring timelines are estimates and can vary based on role and market conditions
Does not replace legal or financial advice for equity compensation

use_cases.title: Early-Stage Founder Hiring Plan
use_cases.description: Founders at pre-seed stage planning their first 5-10 hires with salary and equity allocation
use_cases.target_user: First-time startup founders building initial team

use_cases.title: Series A Headcount Budget
use_cases.description: VP of Finance planning $2M-$10M ARR stage hiring budget and organizational structure

use_cases.title: Equity Pool Design
use_cases.description: Founders determining option pool size and equity grants for executive team hires
use_cases.target_user: Founders preparing for seed or Series A fundraising

prompt_templates.title: Basic Team Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm a pre-seed founder with $500K raised. What should my team look like for the next 12 months?
prompt_templates.scenario: Early-stage founder seeking initial hiring guidance

prompt_templates.title: Hiring Plan with Budget
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a hiring plan for a seed-stage SaaS company targeting $1M ARR. Include roles, salaries, equity, and timeline.

prompt_templates.title: Compensation Benchmarking
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the salary and equity ranges for a VP of Engineering at Series A stage in Austin?

prompt_templates.title: Org Chart Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design the organizational structure for a 30-person startup at Series A with optimal reporting lines.

output_examples.input: What should my engineering team look like at seed stage?
output_examples.output: At seed stage ($500K-$2M ARR), your engineering team should be 4-6 people total. This includes an Engineering Lead ($150K-$180K), 2-3 Full-Stack Engineers ($120K-$150K each), and optionally a Frontend or Backend Specialist ($130K-$160K). Expect 6-8 weeks to hire each engineer, and 2-4 months for them to reach full productivity. Fully-loaded costs (salary × 1.3) will range from $156K-$234K per engineer.

output_examples.input: How much equity should I offer my first VP-level hire?
output_examples.output: For VP-level hires at seed stage, offer 0.5-1.5% equity. This vests over 4 years with a 1-year cliff. The higher end of the range applies to VPs joining very early (pre-seed to seed), while the lower end is appropriate for later seed hires. Factor this into your 10-15% option pool planning to ensure you have enough room for future hires.

best_practices: Multiply base salary by 1.3-1.4 to calculate fully-loaded costs including payroll taxes, benefits, and overhead
Add 2-3 months buffer to hiring timelines to account for recruitment delays and ramp-up time
Reserve 10-15% option pool per funding round (pre-seed through Series A) to ensure adequate equity for hires
anti_patterns: Hiring senior roles too early before validating product-market fit and repeatable sales
Offering equity at the top of ranges without considering stage dilution and pool sustainability
Planning hiring based on idealized timelines without accounting for 6-24 week recruiting cycles

faq.question: What stage does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports startups from pre-seed (0-$500K ARR) through Series A ($2M-$10M ARR), covering team sizes from 2-5 people up to 15-50 people.

faq.question: Are the compensation benchmarks current?
faq.answer: Salary benchmarks are based on 2024 US market rates. Adjust by location: SF/NYC +20-30%, Seattle/Boston/LA +10-20%, Austin/Denver/Chicago +0-10%, remote -10-20%.

faq.question: How do I calculate fully-loaded costs?
faq.answer: Multiply base salary by 1.3-1.4 to account for payroll taxes (7.65%), benefits ($10K-$15K per employee), and overhead ($5K-$10K per employee for workspace, equipment, software).

faq.question: What equity should I offer early employees?
faq.answer: First engineer: 0.5-2.0%. First 5 employees: 0.25-1.0% each. Seed stage VPs: 0.5-1.5%. All equity vests over 4 years with a 1-year cliff.

faq.question: How long does hiring take?
faq.answer: Junior roles: 6-8 weeks. Mid-level: 8-12 weeks. Senior: 12-16 weeks. Executive: 16-24 weeks. Add 2-4 months for productivity ramp-up after hiring.

faq.question: Should I hire full-time or contractors?
faq.answer: Use full-time for core product development, sales, and mission-critical roles. Use contractors for specialized short-term needs (legal, accounting), variable workload (design, marketing), and to test roles before hiring full-time.
